segments.s656-g291: چو آن دید is a temporal clause: چو + آن + دید = 'when (he) saw that.' بنشست is the 3rd singular past of بنشستن ('to sit down'); here the verb precedes the proper name بوزرجمهر (verb–subject inversion typical in poetry), so the phrase reads 'when he saw that, Bozorgmehr sat down.'
segments.s656-g292: همه موبدان is a subject phrase 'all the mōbeds.' برگشادند is the plural past (بر‑ + گشادند) meaning 'they opened/revealed.' چهر (face/countenance) is the direct object; the clause thus means they 'opened/revealed their faces' (i.e. lifted/turned their faces). Note V follows S here (poetic order).
segments.s656-g293: بگسترد is 3rd singular past of گستردن ('he spread/laid out'). پیش اندرون تخت is a prepositional phrase 'before/in front of the inner throne' (پیش = before/in the presence of; اندرون = inner, تخت = throne). نرد (backgammon board) is the object; word order places the object after the locative phrase for poetic effect.
segments.s656-g294: همه گردش مهرها یاد کرد is a compound where همه گردش مهرها ('all the turns/movements of the pieces') is the object of یاد کرد ('he recalled/remembered' — یاد + کردن, with کرد as the light verb). گردش = 'turns/circuits' and یاد کرد is the past tense verbal phrase 'he remembered/recounted.' Here مهرها refers to the game pieces associated with نرد (the backgammon board).
segments.s656-g295: سپهدار بنمود is a verb phrase: سپهدار ('commander') is the direct object or item 'shown' and بنمود (3rd sg. past of بنمودن) = 'he displayed/shown.' The conjunction و links the next noun phrase; جنگ سپاه is a genitive construction 'the war/battle of the army' (جنگ + سپاه = 'the army's battle' or 'the battle/army').
segments.s656-g296: هم آرایش رزم و فرمان شاه: هم = 'also/likewise.' آرایش here means 'array/arrangement' (of troops), rزم = 'battle/war' so آرایش رزم = 'battle formation/array.' فرمان شاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's command(s).' The whole segment lists further things that were presented/described.
segments.s656-g41: کسی کو is an archaic relative phrase meaning “someone who” (کسی + the archaic relative/particle کو). بدانش is a prepositional/ adverbial phrase here meaning “for/with that knowledge.” برد is the past-root form of بردن used here to mean “bore/ endured” (3rd-person), so برد رنج بیش literally ‘bore/undertook greater hardship’ (= ‘took great pains’). بیش modifies رنج as ‘more/greater hardship.’
segments.s656-g42: بفرمای is an honorific/past narrative verb meaning ‘he commanded/ordered’ (here reporting the ruler’s order). تا introduces a purpose/result clause ‘so that/to the end that.’ تخت شطرنج is the compound ‘chess board/board of chess’ (تخت used as a platform/board), and پیش is an adverb ‘before/in front (of him).’ Word order places the object (تخت شطرنج) before the locative/ direction (پیش).
segments.s656-g43: نهند is a form of نهادن (here 3rd-person plural) meaning ‘they placed/put’ (narrative: they set the pieces). The conjunction و links the verbs. ز introduces the source/measure ‘from’ or ‘of,’ هر گونه رای ‘every kind of counsel/opinion.’ آورند is 3rd-person plural ‘they bring/offer,’ here ‘they offered/produced counsel.’
segments.s656-g44: که heads a subordinate/result clause (‘so that/that’). این نغز بازی is ‘this witty/delightful game,’ where نغز qualifies بازی. به جای آوردند is an idiomatic verbal phrase: به جای آوردن = ‘to bring about/perform/execute’; آورند is 3rd-person plural (subjunctive/narrative) meaning ‘they brought about’ or ‘they enacted.’
segments.s656-g45: بدانند is the verb دانستن in a jussive/subjunctive or prescriptive sense here: ‘they should know’ or ‘let them know.’ هرمهره‌ای (‘هر مهره‌ای’) means ‘each piece’ (every chess piece). را is the definite-object/ topical marker. به نام is the idiom ‘by name’ (به + نام = ‘called/by the name’).
segments.s656-g46: که introduces the reported/question clause. گویند here is the verb ‘they say’ (3rd-person plural) used to report speech, though the provided sense-list lacked the verbal sense. پس is ‘then/after that.’ خانه او کدام is an interrogative/relative phrase ‘which is its house/square?’ (خانه here = the piece’s square or place on the board). The clause as a whole: ‘and then they ask/say: which is its square?’
segments.s656-g47: پیاده here names the pawn/foot-soldier piece (literally ‘infantry/pawn’). بدانند again is ‘they should know’ (prescriptive). The list continues with پیل (‘elephant’ — a chess piece in shatranj) and سپاه (‘host/army’), joined by و as simple coordination.
segments.s656-g48: رخ here names a chess piece (the rook) though the supplied senses give only ‘face/appearance,’ so the specialized chess sense is not in the list. واسب (واسب) is ‘horse/steed’ (the knight). رفتار literally means ‘behavior/manner’ and here refers to ‘the moves’ or ways the pieces move; فرزین is the vizier/minister piece (a specialized noun; the supplied senses do not list the chess sense). The final و شاه names the king. The segment is a coordinated list of piece-names and the moves of the vizier and king.
segments.s656-g161: چگونه is the interrogative 'how'. فراز here is an adverbial/poetic element 'up, on high' placed before the verb; آمدش is the past stem آمد + enclitic -ش 'to him/it' meaning 'came to him'. رای این ('this plan/opinion') is the thing that 'rose' into him; word order is poetic (adverb + verb + indirect object + object phrase).
segments.s656-g162: به گیتی is a locative phrase 'in/for the world'. نگیرد is a negative present-form of گرفت#گیر ('take/seize') used here in the sense 'does not/cannot take' (classical Persian allows this present negative to state general impossibility). کسی is indefinite used with the negation to mean 'no one', and جای این 'the place/position of this' is the object whose rival no one in the world can occupy.
segments.s656-g163: چنان گشت is a fixed phrase 'became thus/so' (چنان = 'so'), with گشت the perfective/past of گشتن 'became/turned'. کسری is the subject (Kisra), and ز بوزرجمهر ('because of/from Bozorgmehr') indicates the cause or source—'Kisra became so on account of Bozorgmehr.'
segments.s656-g164: که introduces a result/relative clause. گفتی here is idiomatic: not a literal second-person past but an impersonal 'one would say' or 'as they say' (the lexical entry listed as 'conversation' is being used idiomatically). بدوبخت is an adjective 'wretched'; بنمود is بنمودن (to show/make appear) in the past 'he displayed/manifested'; چهر (face) is the direct object — 'he displayed a wretched face.'
segments.s656-g165: یکی جام is the object 'a certain cup' (یکی = 'a/certain'). فرمود is a past, honorific/authoritative verb here 'ordered/commanded' (often used for royal speech or commands). پس 'then' gives temporal sequencing, and شهریار 'the king' is the subject placed after the verb for poetic order/emphasis.
segments.s656-g166: که begins a relative clause modifying جام. کردند is past plural 'they made/did' (implied agents are attendants). پرگوهر and شاهوار are postpositive adjectives 'jeweled/gem-filled' and 'regal/lofty' describing what they made the cup into.
segments.s656-g167: یکی introduces an item 'one/a certain' (here introducing the gift). بدره is a poetic word ('full moon') used metaphorically as a large or splendid thing (here likely qualifying the sum of money). دینار is the coin, and واسبی is 'a horse' (literally 'an equine'); به زین 'with a saddle' (به + زین indicates the horse is saddled). The sequence lists the gifts in poetic order.
segments.s656-g168: بدو 'to him' is the indirect-object pronoun. داد is the simple past 'gave'. و is the coordinating 'and'. کردش is کرد + enclitic -ش 'to him' and here governs آفرین: 'he made/do him (the) praise' — i.e. 'he praised him'. بسی is an adverb 'greatly/much' and آفرین is 'praise/acclamation' (he bestowed much praise on him).
segments.s656-g73: This is a past‑tense plural clause: verb بپرداختند (past 3pl) at the end governs the earlier noun phrase. یکی here introduces a single/unspecified item ('a certain' / 'one') modifying خرم ایوان; word order (Determiner - Adj - Noun - Verb) is typical of Persian poetry. بپرداختند is an older/poetic past plural meaning ‘set up / attended to’ rather than financial paying.
segments.s656-g74: فرستاده را is a marked definite object (را = object marker). پایگه ساختند is a verb phrase (ساختند past 3pl ‘they made/constructed’) with پایگه as its direct object; the clause means they established a base/station for the envoy. ساختند is the regular past plural of ساختن ‘to make/build’.
segments.s656-g75: This noun phrase links several nouns with و (and). موبدان is the priestly class (mobeds). نماینده is an appositive/noun meaning 'representative' and راه attaches as the noun governed by نماینده ('representative for/on the road' or 'representative on the way'); the whole functions as the subject of the following verb. The sequence is typical in epic style where several nouns are piled before the verb.
segments.s656-g76: برفتند is past plural 'they went/they set off'. یک سر is an adverbial expression here meaning 'straightaway/at once' or 'directly' (literally 'one head' used idiomatically). به نزدیک شاه is the directional phrase 'toward/near the king' (به = to/towards + نزدیک = near + شاه = king). Word order places the verb before the adverbial phrase for poetic effect.
segments.s656-g77: نهادند is a past‑tense, 3rd‑person plural verb 'they placed/set' (نهاد#نه). پس here follows the verb and functions adverbially as 'then'. تخت شطرنج is a noun phrase ('chessboard') where تخت is the board/flat surface and شطرنج specifies the game; پیش is a locational adverb meaning 'before/in front of' (i.e., placed before someone). The word order (verb — adverb — object — locative) is typical of Persian poetic flexibility.
segments.s656-g78: نگه کرد is a compound-like expression with نگه (keeping/retaining) + کرد (did/made) giving the sense 'kept/held' in past, 3rd‑person plural. هریک 'each (one)' is the subject/topic. ز here is the poetic preposition 'from/than' marking the standard for comparison, اندازه 'measure/extent' names the measure, and بیش 'more' completes the comparative ('more than the measure'). Together: 'each kept more than the measure.'
segments.s656-g79: بجستند is past, 3rd‑person plural and here means 'they sprang/jumped' (figuratively 'they leapt into action'). و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. هر گونه‌ای is an expression 'every kind/type' (گونه‌ای is an indefinite/qualifying noun meaning 'a kind' or 'manner'). ساختند is past, 3rd‑person plural 'they made/devised' — together: 'they sprang and devised every kind (of move).'
segments.s656-g80: ز هر دست is a prepositional phrase 'from each hand' (ز = 'from', هر = 'each', دست = 'hand'). یکبارش is composed of یکبار 'one time/one (turn)' plus the enclitic -ش 'it/of it', meaning 'one of them / one at a time'. انداختند is past, 3rd‑person plural 'they threw/cast'. The whole clause: 'from each hand they threw one (of them) at a time.'
segments.s656-g209: ز + noun phrase ('ز گنج شهنشاه') is a prepositional phrase meaning 'from the Shahanshah's treasure' (classical/poetic word order omits the ezafe and places the prepositional phrase up front). کردند is the past plural 'they made/caused' and بار is the direct object 'a load/parcel' placed after the verb because of the poem's inverted order.
segments.s656-g210: بشد is the past/impersonal sense 'it became/there came to be' (here: 'a caravan came into being / set out'); کاروان is the subject 'caravan'; از + در (در = 'door, gate' rather than just 'in') gives 'from the king's gate' with شهریار in genitive — again the poem omits an explicit ezafe.
segments.s656-g211: چوشد is the contracted poetic sequence 'چو شد' = 'when/once'; بارهای شتر is a noun phrase 'the loads of the camels' (بارهای = plural 'loads') and ساخته is a past participle/adjectival form 'made/packed' modifying those loads ('the camel-loads having been made').
segments.s656-g212: دلِ شاه 'the king's heart' is the topic; زان = 'from/with that' (referring back to the completed task) and پرداخته is a past participle of پرداختن used here to mean 'has turned to / applied itself to / been occupied with' that matter — i.e., the king's mind was set on that affair.
segments.s656-g213: فرستاده is the subject 'the envoy (the one sent)'; رای را is the definite object 'the counsel/plan' marked by را; پیش here means 'before / in the presence of' (i.e., read aloud before the king); خواند is the past tense 'he read / recited'. The clause order (subject — object — adverb — verb) is poetic but all elements function as in New Persian.
segments.s656-g214: ز دانش فراوان is an adverbial phrase 'from/with abundant knowledge' qualifying how the following utterances are produced; سخنها is plural 'words / speeches'; براند is a poetic/archaic verb form meaning 'he put forth / produced / drove forth' (i.e., 'uttered').
segments.s656-g215: یکی is the indefinite 'a certain' (someone); نامه is 'a letter' as direct object; بنوشت is the poetic/archaic past 'wrote' (the element is listed oddly in the word data — here it functions as the verb 'wrote'); نزدیک اوی is an adverbial phrase 'near him' (اوی = archaic he/him). Word order is typical of classical verse: indefinite + object + verb + adverbial.
segments.s656-g216: پر از is the fixed phrase 'full of'; it takes a list (دانش و رامش و رنگ و بوی) joined by و. These nouns list qualities of the letter: دانش 'knowledge/learning', رامش 'tranquility/solace', رنگ 'color/appearance', بوی 'scent' — the last two are a poetic collocation describing style and fragrance.
segments.s656-g193: برین گونه is a frozen phrase meaning “in this way” (بر + این → “on/this”). تا introduces a temporal clause “until/when”; که بودی is an archaic past relative clause “who/that was” (بودی = was). شکن is a noun “breaker/one who breaks” (here = ‘the clash/one who breaks the ranks’). This whole clause sets the time/condition for the main verb that follows.
segments.s656-g194: شدندی is an archaic past plural “they became / they came to be” (verb before subject is common in epic poetry). The subject follows: دو شاه و سپاه انجمن = “the two kings and the assembled host/army” (انجمن = ‘assembled’/‘gathered’ modifying سپاه). Together the segment says these parties came into being/appeared at the time set by the previous clause.
segments.s656-g195: بدین سان که گفتم = “in the way I said” (بدین سان = “thus/in this manner”; که گفتم is a relative clause “which I said”). بیاراست is a past verb “arranged/set up” (آراستن in poetic form) and نرد is the direct object “the backgammon board.” Word order here is typical poetic V + object after the qualifying phrase.
segments.s656-g196: برشاه شد is a common poetic passive/impersonal sequence “it was put before the king / it became on the king” (برشاه = ‘before/on the king’; شد = ‘became/was done’). یک به یک is an adverbial “one by one,” and یاد کرد is a light-verb construction “remembered/considered” (یاد + کردن = to recall/attend to). Together: the board was set before the king and he inspected it one by one.
segments.s656-g197: وزان = “from that / concerning that” (a poetic prepositional particle). رفتن is a verbal noun here meaning “conduct/way of behaving” (the noun form of ‘to go’ used idiomatically), and شاه برترمنش = “the high‑minded / haughty king” (برترمنش = proud). The phrase frames the topic whose consequences follow.
segments.s656-g198: همانش ... همان ... is a tersely parallel idiom: همانش ستایش همان سرزنش = “the same thing was praise, the same thing reproach.” همانش (the same) + ستایش (praise) and then همان + سرزنش (reproach) expresses that the same conduct earned both praise and blame.
segments.s656-g199: ز is a poetic preposition “by/from” indicating means or cause. نیروی و فرمان و جنگ سپاه is a triadic list of instruments/grounds — “by strength, by command, and by the warfare (battle), the army…” — preparing the clause that follows. The nouns are linked as coordinated means.
segments.s656-g200: بگسترد is an older past form “he spread/deployed” (ب‑ prefix + گسترد). و بنمود = “and he displayed/shown” (بنمود = showed/put on view). یک‌یک is “one by one,” and بشاه = “to the king.” Together: he deployed the army and presented each part, one by one, to the king.
segments.s656-g217: سر نامه is a literary phrase meaning “at the head/top of the book” (سر = top + نامه = book). کرد is the past tense verb “made/wrote” and takes the following noun phrase آفرین بزرگ (“a great praise”) as its object; آفرین here is a noun (an acclamation/praise), not the verbal stem “to create.” Word order places the location/setting (سر نامه) frontally, then the verb+object.
segments.s656-g218: به یزدان پناهش z-marked phrase: به introduces the beneficiary/recipient “to/for God” (یزدان = God). پناهش is a possessive noun (“its/his refuge”); the following ز (archaic poetic 'from') introduces the source/agent: ز دیو سترگ = “from/of the ... demon.” Note سترگ here is an archaic adjective meaning “mighty/large” modifying دیو; the precise sense of سترگ is not present in the supplied sense list.
segments.s656-g219: دگر گفت is a simple past construction meaning “again/then said” (دگر = again/another; گفت = said). کای is an archaic vocative particle (”O”) addressing the addressee; it is followed by the apposition نامور شاه هند = “famed/renowned king of India.” The vocative (کای) precedes the title, which is typical in classical Persian address.
segments.s656-g220: ز ... تا is the common poetic pair meaning “from ... to.” دریای قنوج is a genitive phrase (“the sea of Qannauj/Kannauj”); تا پیش سند means “as far as/up to before Sind” (پیش here = in front of/bordering on). The whole chunk gives a geographic extent.
segments.s656-g221: رسیداین is an archaic/inverted order meaning “arrived” (literally ‘came these’); the verb is fronted here for poetic effect. فرستاده رای‌زن is a noun phrase “messengers/couriers and counselors” (فرستاده = envoy/messenger; رای‌زن = adviser/counselor) and is the subject of the verb. The verb-subject order reflects classical poetic flexibility.
segments.s656-g222: ابا ... بدین انجمن functions as an adverbial/qualifier: ابا here is a poetic/archaic form equivalent to با (“with”), introducing accompaniment (with canopies and elephants). چتر و پیلان = “canopies (or canopied umbrellas) and elephants”; بدین انجمن = به + این انجمن (“into/to this assembly”) where بدین = به + دین/این (poetic fusion). The whole phrase describes how they arrived.
segments.s656-g223: همان is the demonstrative “that/same” modifying تخت شطرنج, a compound meaning literally “throne of chess” or “the chessboard (set).” The phrase پیغام رای is a noun (پیغام = message) qualified by رای = “counsel/plan,” giving “the message of counsel.” The nouns are listed before the verb, as is common in Persian narration.
segments.s656-g224: شنیدیم is the simple past plural “we heard.” و links the clauses, and پیغامش (پیغام + possessive -ش) “his/its message” is the object/topic of the next verb. امد is the past tense of آمدن “came/arrived.” بجای is a fixed adverbial meaning “into place/in due place” (i.e., the message arrived and took effect). Together: “we heard ... and his message arrived in its place.”
segments.s656-g345: This begins with the noun ببر (subject) ‘a tiger’, followed by the locative preposition در ‘in/at’, and the verb گرفتَش ‘seized him’ where the enclitic ـش is the 3rd-person singular object pronoun ‘him/it’. The following words جهاندار شاه are a nominative title that belongs to the next clause (they name the speaker/agent that will have the verb in the next segment).
segments.s656-g346: بپرسیدش is a past (classical) third-person form ‘he asked’ with the enclitic ـش ‘him’ as object; از is the preposition ‘about/concerning’ governing the following nouns. رای here means ‘counsel/plan’ (a decision or advice). وز in this construction functions as a poetic/linking particle (essentially joining nouns: ‘and’), not its literal senses listed below. رنج راه = ‘the hardship/burden of the road’; راه = ‘road/path’. The whole clause: ‘the king asked him about his counsel and the hardships of the road.’
segments.s656-g347: بگفت is the past narrative verb ‘he said’ and introduces reported speech. آنکجا is an adverb ‘to that place/there’ (or ‘thereupon’), رفت is the past of رفتن ‘went’, and بوزرجمهر is the proper name (Bozorgmehr) as the subject of رفت. Word order is typical of classical Persian reporting: ‘he said (that) Bozorgmehr went there.’
segments.s656-g348: ازان is a compound prepositional/adverbial ‘from that’ or ‘by that’. بخت here denotes ‘fate/destiny’ and بیدار is an adjective ‘awake/roused’ (i.e. ‘roused by fate’). و is the simple coordinating conjunction ‘and’. مهر followed by سپهر is an idiomatic phrase meaning ‘the favour/kindness of heaven’ (مهر = favour/kindness, سپهر = the heavens). Together: ‘by that roused fate and by heaven’s favour.’
segments.s656-g349: پس is a discourse adverb ‘then/so’. آن is the demonstrative ‘that’, نامه is ‘letter’ and رای here means ‘counsel/plan’ — together ‘that letter of counsel’. پیروزبخت is an adjective ‘fortunate/auspicious’ modifying the noun phrase (i.e. ‘the auspicious/fortunate letter’).
segments.s656-g350: بیاورد is a past-form ‘he brought’ (classical usage), و = ‘and’. بنهاد is a past verb meaning ‘he put/placed/laid (it down)’ — the direct object (the letter) is omitted because it was just mentioned. در پیش تخت is a locative phrase ‘before/in the presence of the throne’ (در = in/at, پیش = before/in the presence of, تخت = throne).
segments.s656-g351: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb often used for sovereign speech and here functions as ‘he commanded/ordered/said’. تا introduces a subordinate clause of purpose or command ‘that/so that’. یزدگرد دبیر is the name plus title ‘Yazdegerd the scribe/secretary’ — the person being summoned.
segments.s656-g352: بیامد is the past form ‘he came’. بر is the preposition here meaning ‘before/toward/in the presence of’. شاه is the noun ‘king’, and دانش‌پذیر is an epithet following شاه meaning ‘receptive to knowledge’ or ‘a king who accepts learning’ (an attributive epithet modifying شاه).
segments.s656-g153: چو introduces a temporal subordinate clause meaning 'when'. Word order is verb-final poetic Persian: subject بوزرجمهر appears before the object, the definite object marker را follows سپه, and the verb براند (a past narrative form) comes at the end meaning 'drove away' or 'routed'.
segments.s656-g154: همه انجمن is the noun phrase 'the whole assembly/everyone'. درشگفتی is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'in astonishment'. بماند is used here as a past/narrative verb 'remained' (the b- prefix appears in poetic narrative forms).
segments.s656-g155: The clause has a reversed order typical of poetry: the predicate غمی شد 'became sorrow' appears before the logical subject فرستاده هند 'the Indian messenger/envoy'. سخت is an adverbial intensifier meaning 'severely/deeply' modifying 'became sorrowful'.
segments.s656-g156: بماند is again a past/narrative verb 'remained'. اندر آن کار 'in that matter' is an adverbial phrase, and هشیار بخت is a noun phrase 'watchful/alert Fate' (هشیار qualifying بخت); word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect, with the subject following the locative phrase.
segments.s656-g157: شگفت اندرو is a verbless/exclamatory phrase 'there was a marvel in that' or 'it was wondrous'. The following clause مرد جادو بماند means 'the magician/sorcerer remained'; again بماند functions as a past/narrative verb. اندرو ('in it/therein') links the wonder to the situation just described.
segments.s656-g158: دلش را is the object 'his heart/mind' (دل with the possessive clitic ش and object marker را). به اندیشه اندر نشاند is a poetic expression 'he placed/settled it into thought' — نشاند is an archaic/perfective past meaning 'set/placed' (here 'devoted/turned his heart to thinking').
segments.s656-g159: که here introduces an explanatory/subordinate clause 'for/that'. این تخت شطرنج is 'this chess-board' (تخت used as a flat platform/board). هرگز ندید is a generic negative 'never was seen' (subject unspecified — 'no one ever saw it').
segments.s656-g160: The segment begins with the adversative/negative نه 'nor' (not listed among the word tokens) and continues از کاردانان هندی شنید 'was heard of from the Indian experts' — meaning the experts of India had not heard of it either. شنید here has the sense 'hear of / learn about'.
segments.s656-g33: This is a subordinate clause introduced by the preposition/particle ز meaning 'because of'. که begins a relative clause 'that/which', so the clause reads 'because of something which ...'. برد here functions as a noun-like verbal notion 'gain/what was obtained' (poetic usage), and اندرَان ('therein') + رای ('counsel/plan') locates the action 'in that council'. رنج is the direct object carried forward into the next segment (i.e. 'brought grief'). Word order is typical older Persian: particle + head noun + relative clause + location + object.
segments.s656-g34: فرستاد is the main verb of the sentence 'sent/dispatched'; its subject (کسری, Khosrow) follows it (a common poetic word order). سراسر به گنج is an adverbial phrase 'throughout the treasury' describing the extent/place of what was sent. Grammatically, the object of فرستاد is the رنج (grief/hardship) mentioned at the end of the previous segment.
segments.s656-g35: بیاورد is a past-tense verb 'he brought/caused to be brought'; پس 'then' signals sequence. نامه‌ای is the direct object 'a letter' and بر پرند literally 'on a bird' (i.e. attached to a bird or carried by a bird). The phrase thus means he caused a letter to be brought on/with a bird.
segments.s656-g36: نبشته here = 'inscription' or 'what was written' (i.e. the text on the letter/bird). بنوشین‌روان is a fixed poetic/exhortative phrase understood as an address or imperative 'Drink, O soul' (used here as the opening words of the inscription). رای هند 'the counsel/plan of India' specifies the content of that inscription. The order places the inscription word(s) after the mention of the written object.
segments.s656-g37: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain' or 'someone' introducing an agent. تخت شطرنج is the compound 'chess-board' (تخت = board/platform, شطرنج = chessboard here) and کرده is the perfect participle 'has made' (past action). به رنج is an adverbial phrase 'with toil/at great cost' describing how the board was made.
segments.s656-g38: This clause continues the result: تهی کرده 'has emptied' (here 'made (the treasury) empty / spent it') takes از رنج 'because of the toil/expense' as the causal phrase. شطرنج qualifies that toil ('the toil/expense of the chess') and گنج is the locus 'the treasury' that was emptied. Word order places cause before the thing emptied and is typical of older Persian syntax.
segments.s656-g39: بیاورد 'he brought' is the main verb; پیغام هندی is the object 'an Indian message'. ز رای here means 'prompted by / on account of the plan/counsel' (z + abstract noun), i.e. the message was brought because of that counsel.
segments.s656-g40: که introduces the content/purpose clause 'that/so that'. تا چرخ باشد is a temporal/conditional phrase 'so long as the sky/heavens (or fate) remain' (تا = 'as long as/until'). تو is the addressee 'you' and بادی (a poetic adjective/participial sense 'borne by the wind' or 'wind‑borne') is the wished-for state; به جای 'to your place' is the destination. The whole clause reads as a conventional blessing/wish introduced by the message.
segments.s656-g65: بپرسید is a past, 3rd‑person singular verb (classical Persian form) meaning “asked.” ازو is the older fused form of از او (“from him / of him”) and functions as the verbal object. شاه پیروزبخت is the subject placed after the verb (V + object + subject order is common in older Persian); پیروزبخت is an epithet modifying شاه (“the fortunate king”).
segments.s656-g66: ازان = از آن (“from/about that”) introduces the list of items. The nouns joined by و enumerate what is being asked about: پیکر (here = figure/shape), مهره (amulet/bead), مشک (musk), and تخت (here = the throne). The conjunction و simply links the items.
segments.s656-g67: چنین (“thus”) modifies the whole reply. داد + پاسخ literally “gave answer” (past tense verb داد + noun پاسخ = ‘replied’). که introduces the quoted speech that follows; ای شهریار is a vocative (“O king/sovereign”) addressing the addressee inside the quotation.
segments.s656-g68: همه (“all”) quantifies the compound رسم و راه (“custom and way/manner”), a fixed collocation meaning ‘rules/manners and methods.’ از در کارزار is a prepositional phrase: از (here = “of/about”) + در (literally “door/gate” but idiomatically part of the locative phrase) + کارزار (“battlefield”), so the sense is ‘belonging to / arising from the battlefield.’
segments.s656-g69: ببینی is the imperfect/present (classical) 2nd‑person form meaning “you will see / you see.” چویابی = چو + یابی = temporal clause “when you find / when you come upon” (چو = when, یابی = you find). به بازیش راه literally = “the way in its play” where بازیش is بازى + ـش (“its play/doing”) and راه = “way/manner”; the phrase means “the manner in which it acts/plays.”
segments.s656-g70: A simple noun list joined by و: رخ (here = appearance/visage/spectacle), پیل (elephant, i.e. the war‑elephant), و آرایش رزمگاه = “and the array/deployment of the battlefield” (آرایش = arrangement/array; رزمگاه = battlefield/place of fight). The trio describes components of the battle’s display/formation.
segments.s656-g71: بدو = به او (“to him”) is an indirect‑object phrase. گفت here functions as the past verb “said” (classical narrative). یک هفته ... زمان = “one week’s time” (یک هفته = one week; زمان = time/period) with ما را marking the beneficiary/affected party (“for us” / “to us”), i.e. “give us a week’s time.”
segments.s656-g72: ببازیم is a 1st‑person plural verbal form meaning something like “we will [engage/meet/contest] / we will take up (the affair)” — the exact lexical mapping is obscure in the provided senses, but here it continues the request: “on the eighth we will ...”. هشتم (the eighth) is the temporal noun “on the eighth [day].” به روشن‌روان can be read as “against/with Roshan‑ravan” or as naming the person involved (روشن‌روان = a proper epithet/name ‘Bright‑soul’). The phrase therefore says: “on the eighth, with Roshan‑ravan.”
segments.s656-g233: نهادیم is the first-person plural past of نهادن (“to place/set”), so “we placed.” برجای here functions as a prepositional phrase meaning “in the place of / instead of” and precedes the nouns it links. The sequence شطرنج نرد is a compressed object phrase giving the thing replaced (شطرنج, chess) followed by the thing put in its stead (نرد, backgammon).
segments.s656-g234: کنون is a temporal adverb “now.” تا here marks purpose (so that / in order that). به بازی is a prepositional phrase literally “in/at the play/game.” که introduces a relative clause; آرد is the 3rd-person singular present/present-subjunctive of آوردن (“brings”), and نبرد functions as a noun (“battle/struggle”) as the thing that the game is said to bring.
segments.s656-g235: برهمن is the noun “Brahmin” or wise/ascetic man. فراوان after the noun indicates abundance (“many/much”). بود is the past copula “was/were.” پاک‌رای is a compound adjective meaning “of pure reputation / of pure opinion” describing those Brahmins.
segments.s656-g236: که introduces the content clause (“who/that said/held that”). این بازی is the subject “this game.” آرد (brings) is the verb (3rd sg. of آوردن). به دانش به جای is a short idiomatic phrase: به دانش marks the goal/object (“to learning”), and به جای here functions idiomatically as “in place (of) / instead,” so the clause says the game “brings learning instead (of something).”
segments.s656-g237: ز is the poetic preposition “from / because of.” چیزی که دید is a relative phrase “something that (he) saw” where که introduces the relative clause and دید is the past “saw.” این فرستاده is the noun phrase “this messenger.” رنج here is a noun “suffering/pain/offense,” used as the affected state of the messenger (elliptically: he felt pain/offense).
segments.s656-g238: فرستد is the verb “sent” (classical poetic form; here used for past action). همه رای هندی is the object: همه “all,” رای in the sense “counsel/plan,” qualified by هندی “Indian.” به گنج is a prepositional phrase marking destination “to the treasury/treasure.”
segments.s656-g239: ور is a conditional particle meaning “if.” ایدون is a verbal form meaning “they come/appear” (classical usage). کجا رای is an interrogative/relative phrase literally “where (is) the counsel” or “whither the counsel,” with رای = “counsel/plan.” با راهنمای is a prepositional phrase “with a guide,” where با = “with” and راهنمای = “guide.”
segments.s656-g240: بکوشند is the present/subjunctive 3rd-person plural of کوشیدن with the prefix ب‑ (“they try/endeavor”). بازی is the noun “the game.” نیاید is the negative present/subjunctive/optative of آمدن (“does not come/ will not come / does not fit”). به جای is a fixed phrase “to its place / rightly / in order,” so the clause means the game will not come/fit into its proper place (i.e. will not succeed or be appropriate).
segments.s656-g25: فراوان is an adverb placed before the verb to mark quantity (‘in abundance’). ببار is an imperative/jussive form meaning ‘pour/let fall’ (hortative). اندرون is a locative/advective noun meaning ‘inside/within’ and introduces the goal; سیم و زر (here represented by زر in the word list) are the direct objects: ‘silver and gold’ being poured into the interior.
segments.s656-g26: This is a paired correlative introduced by چه ... چه ... meaning ‘whether ... or ...’; each چه plus از introduces a source/material. از is the preposition ‘from/of’; مشک و عنبر and (second) از عود‌تر enumerate perfumes/odors. عود‌تر is a poetic form of عود (oud/agarwood).
segments.s656-g27: ز is the poetic preposition ‘from’. یاقوت والماس lists gems (‘ruby’ and a poetic term for jewels). وز here is written as a single token (classical contraction of و + ز) functioning to link clauses (‘and/from’), so it connects the following item تیغ هند (‘blades/swords of India’). هند marks origin ‘India’.
segments.s656-g28: همه is a universal quantifier ‘all/every’. تیغ هندی is the noun phrase ‘Indian sword(s)’ with هندی as an adjectival/relational adjective. سراسر is an adverb meaning ‘throughout/entirely’ modifying the predicate. پرند here is a predicate noun (poetic) often glossed as ‘winged one’ or ‘ornamented’, so the phrase says that the Indian swords are wholly so.
segments.s656-g29: ز چیزی introduces an indefinite source ‘from something/whatever’. که is the relative/connector ‘that/which’. خیزد is a present/subjunctive form of خاست ‘to arise/come forth’ (here ‘that comes from…’). The second ز again marks source, قنوج is the place name Kannauj, and رای is a noun ‘plan/counsel’ — so the clause says ‘that comes from Kannauj and counsel/plan’.
segments.s656-g30: زده is a past-participial/adjectival form meaning ‘struck/affected’ and modifies the following nouns. دست و پای is a coordinated object ‘hands and feet’. آوریده is a perfect/past participle ‘brought’ (here resultative: ‘having been brought’). به جای is a locative phrase ‘to the place’ or ‘into position’. The whole unit says that the (hands and feet), struck, were brought to the place.
segments.s656-g31: ببردند is a past-tense, 3rd-person plural verb ‘they took/they carried away’. یک سر همه is a quantified object phrase; here it functions as ‘one head from each/all’ or ‘a single head (from the group)’. پیش تخت is a prepositional phrase ‘before the throne’ (پیش = in the presence of, تخت = throne), indicating the direction/goal of the action.
segments.s656-g32: نگه کرد is a common light-verb construction (نگه + کرد) forming the past ‘he/it kept watch / looked’. سالار خورشید بخت is a titular apposition: سالار ‘commander/chief’ followed by the epithet خورشید بخت ‘sun of fortune’ (a laudatory title for the ruler). Word order places the verb before the title, a normal poetic arrangement.
segments.s656-g169: بشد is an archaic/poetic past-form meaning “became” or “came to be” and here introduces the action; مرد دانا is the subject (noun + adjective). به آرام خویش is an adverbial phrase — با/به + noun + possessive suffix — literally “in/with his own calm,” i.e. “quietly” or “in his calm.” Word order (verb – subject – adverbial) is typical of older Persian poetry.
segments.s656-g170: یکی تخت و پرگار are the direct objects placed by the subject: یکی (indefinite “one/a certain”) + two nouns joined by و. بنهاد is the (poetic) past of نهادن “to put/place” and appears at the end as the main verb; پیش is an adverbial particle “in front (of him).” The subject is carried over from the previous segment.
segments.s656-g171: به marks the domain/target of attention (“with regard to” / “to”), applied to both following nouns. شطرنج و اندیشه هندوان reads as two coordinated items: “chess and the thought/ideas of the Indians” — هندوان is postpositive and works like a genitive/attributive to اندیشه (“the Indians’ thought”).
segments.s656-g172: نگه + کرد is a light/compound verb: نگه (attention/look) + کرد (did) = “he looked/paid attention.” و links the second clause; بفزود (poetic past) = “he increased/added.” رنج روان is a genitive phrase “the suffering of the soul” (رنج = suffering, روان = soul/spirit) and is the object of بفزود.
segments.s656-g173: خرد is the noun “wisdom.” بادل here is written as one word but should be read as با دل (“with heart”), an instrumental phrase meaning “with a heart/feeling”; روشن modifies that phrase (“bright/clear-hearted”). انباز is an archaic/uncertain lexical item (the manuscript/poetic form is obscure) and is governed by کرد, so the sequence means roughly “wisdom, with a clear heart, did/performed anbaz.” کرد is the past tense auxiliary/main verb “did/made.”
segments.s656-g174: به اندیشه بنهاد is an adverbial + verb: به اندیشه = “with thought / thoughtfully,” and بنهاد (poetic past of نهادن) = “placed/put.” برتخت نرد is the locative phrase “on the nard-board” (بر + تخت = “on the board”; نرد = backgammon). The construction shows the action’s manner (with thought) and its target (on the board).
segments.s656-g175: دومهره (two pieces / a pair of pieces) is the object of the order. بفرمود is an honorific/polite past “(he) commanded/ordered.” کردن is the infinitive “to make” (here part of the causative/ordering construction). ز is the poetic preposition “from/of,” and عاج = “ivory.” Together they mean “he ordered a pair of pieces to be made from ivory.”
segments.s656-g176: همه پیکر عاج is “all the ivory figures” (همه = all; پیکر = figure/piece; عاج = ivory). همرنگ ساج is a simile/comparative phrase “the same color as saj” (همرنگ = same-colored; ساج here names the wood/tree whose color is the model). Together: all the ivory pieces should be the color of saj.
segments.s656-g145: چپ و راست is a paired locative phrase 'left and right' placed before the noun it modifies. صف برکشیده is a noun (صف) plus a perfect participle/adjectival form (برکشیده) meaning 'lines drawn up' or 'ranks raised' (no copula is needed in Persian poetry). سوار follows as a nominative/appositive noun specifying who those ranks consisted of: 'horsemen'.
segments.s656-g146: This string lists the foot soldiers and their position. پیاده is a noun/adjective 'infantry, on foot' and stands in parallel to سوار above. به پیش اندرون is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in front of / before the inner [place]'; اندرون is the inner court/quarters. نیزه دار is a compound noun 'نیزه' + agentive 'دار' meaning 'spear‑bearers' (those having spears) — here the agentive دار functions as a bound element forming 'pike/spear‑men'.
segments.s656-g147: هشیوار is an adjective/noun 'watchful, alert'. دستور here functions as a title/agency noun (classical usage = a commander or 'one who gives orders'); the phrase is a nominal group (no explicit verb) followed by در پیش شاه, a set prepositional phrase 'in the presence of / before the king' where در پیش together mean 'before'. The whole phrase reads as an identifying nominative: 'the watchful commander before the king.'
segments.s656-g148: به رزم = 'to/into the battle' (به used directionally). اندرونش combines اندرون 'inner place' with the enclitic -ش 'his/its' = 'his/its inner [place]'. نماینده راه is a nominal compound 'نماینده (one who shows/represents) + راه (way)' = 'guide' or 'path‑finder'. Word order is inverted poetically: the prepositional phrase 'به رزم اندرونش' marks the destination/purpose and 'نماینده راه' names the person — 'the guide into the inner battle.'
segments.s656-g149: مبارز is an agent noun 'fighter/contestant'. که introduces a relative clause 'who…'. اسب is the direct object 'horse' and افگند is the past verb 'threw/caused to fall' (simple past). بر دو روی is a locative/directional phrase with بر + 'دو روی' literally 'on/toward two sides' = 'to either/both sides.' The clause reads: 'the fighter who threw (his) horse to both sides.'
segments.s656-g150: به دست چپش is an instrumental phrase 'with/by his left hand' — به used instrumentally and چپش = چپ + enclitic -ش 'his left'. پیل پرخاشجوی is a noun phrase 'an elephant (پیل) + adjective پرخاشجوی (aggressive)'; in epic diction 'پیل' is often figurative (very large/forceful) and can describe a massive weapon or a mighty force. The noun phrase is juxtaposed without a copula, typical of Persian poetic style.
segments.s656-g151: وزو appears here as an archaic/poetic particle or connective (the supplied senses list does not match this usage); it likely functions to link or position the next phrase ('over them' / 'and above'). برتر modifies the following noun phrase as 'superior' or 'higher.' اسبان جنگی is a compound 'war horses' — اسبان (horses) + جنگی (warlike/war‑horses). به پای is a locative 'at the foot/base of' (به marking location). Overall the segment gives a spatial arrangement: 'and above them the superior war‑horses at the foot…'.
segments.s656-g152: بدان here functions as 'by that / thereby' (ب- prefix + دان forming a deictic), introducing a result/purpose clause. تاکه is the subordinating particle 'so that / until', introducing purpose or result. آید is the present-subjunctive/present form of 'آمدن' ('come'), used here in the sense 'may come / reach.' به بالای رای is a prepositional phrase 'to the top/height (بالای) of رای' — رای here = 'counsel/decision' (i.e. 'the high council' or 'the summit of counsel'). Grammatically this is a purpose/result construction: 'so that thereby it may come to the high counsel.'
segments.s656-g273: This string gives the source and description of the person chosen. به کشور is a locative prepositional phrase ('in/within the realm'); ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; پیران is the plural noun 'elders'; شایسته is an adjective ('worthy') modifying مرد ('man'). Word order in classical/poetic Persian can put the locative phrase first and the modifying adjective before the head noun.
segments.s656-g274: یکی ('one' / 'a certain') introduces the chosen individual; انجمن کرد is a compound where کرد is the past tense verb 'made/did' and انجمن is the object 'an assembly/meeting' ('he convened/gathered an assembly'). و بنهاد links with 'and (he) placed/established' — بنهاد is an archaic past form meaning 'set up' or 'established' — and نرد is the object 'backgammon' (the board/game).
segments.s656-g275: به یک هفته marks duration ('for one week'); آنکس که is a relative clause introducer ('that person who / whoever'); بد is an attributive/ adverbial 'bad' modifying the following noun; تیزویر (here an archaic/obscure compound) functions as a noun meaning 'deceit/treachery' or a sly device. The clause therefore reads 'for a week, whoever was guilty of bad deceit...'.
segments.s656-g276: ازان is 'of those/from those' and links the relative clause to the preceding group; نامداران is 'the famed/renowned ones'; برنا و پیر lists ages/conditions 'young and old' (برنا = youthful/vigorous, پیر = old). Together this specifies the class: 'those renowned, young or old.'
segments.s656-g277: همی‌بازجستند is a literary/poetic verbal form with the progressive prefix همی- meaning 'kept on / continued to' and بازجستند here functioning as 'played' or 'sought play'; بازی نرد is the object phrase 'the game (play) of nard/backgammon.' The verb is plural, referring to the assembled players.
segments.s656-g278: This is a string of motives introduced by prepositions: به رشک 'out of envy/jealousy', و برای 'and for (the sake of) ...', و به ننگ 'and to/for shame', و نبرد 'and (even) for battle/strife.' The coordinating و links the items; برای and به mark purpose/reason.
segments.s656-g279: بهشتم functions as an indirect-object phrase 'to Beheshtam' (here a recipient/name); چنین گفت is 'said thus' (چنین = 'thus, so', گفت = 'said' in a literary register); موبد is the agent 'the mobed / Zoroastrian priest'; به رای means 'in counsel' or 'as advice' (به + رای = 'to/with opinion/counsel'). Word order places the indirect object up front for emphasis.
segments.s656-g280: که introduces the content clause 'that ...'; این را is the object 'this' with the definite object marker را; نداند is a literary negative-present/past form of 'to know' meaning 'knows not' (i.e., 'no one knows'); کسی in negative contexts reads as 'no one'; سر زپای is an idiom 'from head to foot' meaning 'completely' or 'throughout.'
segments.s656-g89: Subject first (من = 'I'), then the noun phrase این نغز بازی ('this witty/delightful game') and finally the verbal phrase به جای آورم. The verb آورم is 1st‑person singular in an older/poetic form (here functioning as a prospective/performative: 'I will/do bring/perform'). به جای آوردن is an idiom meaning 'to accomplish/perform' (به + جای + آوردن). نغز modifies بازی and precedes it as a normal adjective.
segments.s656-g90: خرد را is the definite/direct object (را marks 'wisdom'). بدین رهنمای is a prepositional/agentive phrase (بدین = به + این, 'by/with this') modifying آورم; رهنمای is the noun 'guide/adviser'. The verb آورم repeats the same 1sg verbal form; overall: 'I will bring wisdom by/through this guide.' Note that in classical Persian 'بدین' is written as one word though analysis here split it.
segments.s656-g91: بدو ('to him') is a prepositional/indirect‑object phrase placed before the verb for emphasis. گفت is the reporting verb ('said') in poetic/narrative past; شاه is the agent (subject) placed after the verb (inverted order common in poetry). این سخن کارتست is a copular/nominal clause functioning as the content of what he said: کارتست is an archaic contracted form meaning 'is your business/affair' (کارِ توست → کارتست).
segments.s656-g92: که introduces a content clause ('that' / 'namely'); روشن‌روان is a compound adjective/noun meaning 'clear‑minded, bright‑souled' describing the addressee. بادی here is an archaic optative/verb form from بودن (classical 'باد' + ending) meaning 'may you be' or an exhortation 'be'. و تندرست simply adds 'and healthy'. In short, the clause is an optative/instructive phrase: 'may you be bright‑souled and healthy.'
segments.s656-g93: کنون is a temporal adverb 'now.' رای قنوج is a genitive/compound meaning 'the counsel/opinion of Kannauj' (رای + place name). گوید is the reporting verb 'says' (here narrative present used in reporting speech). که introduces the subordinate clause that follows ('that').
segments.s656-g94: ندارد is the negative present/past existential/possessive construction 'does not have' (here 'has not'). یکی مرد is an idiomatic expression 'one man' = 'any man' / 'a single man' (یکی functions as 'one / someone'). جوینده is a present‑participle/adjective 'seeking' (i.e., 'seeker'), modifying مرد. راه is the noun 'way/path' and completes جوینده as 'a seeker of the way.' Note the verb ندارد precedes the subject phrase for stylistic emphasis.
segments.s656-g95: شکست بزرگ است is a nominal predication 'it is a great defeat' (شکست = defeat/failure; بزرگ = great). بر introduces the target/affected party (بر + accusative/oblique), موبدان is the plural of موبد (mōbed, Zoroastrian priest). The whole phrase states that the priests suffer a great defeat/humiliation.
segments.s656-g96: This segment strings prepositional phrases joined by و: به در گاه ('at/into the court/royal presence' — note classical درگاه is split here into در + گاه), و بر گاه ('and at the assemblies/occasions' — گاه used as 'occasion/assembly' in context), و بر بخردان ('and upon/against the wise' — بخردان = 'the wise' in oblique). Prepositions (به / بر) govern the following nouns; the repetition emphasizes different social settings where the defeat is felt.
segments.s656-g49: گراین functions as a conditional 'if this' (classical/poetic spelling combining گر + این); نغز بازی is a noun phrase 'a witty/delicate play/trick' (adjective + noun) placed before the verb. به جای آوردند is the idiom 'به جای آوردن' meaning 'to carry out / perform (properly)', with آورند as third-person plural (classical present/subjunctive/optative form used in protasis). Word order is subjectless: condition phrase → verb.
segments.s656-g50: درین is the older form of 'در این' = 'in this'; کار 'affair/matter' names the domain. پاکیزه رای آورند uses the verb phrase رای آوردن ('to form/bring an opinion, to decide'), here with پاکیزه as an adverbial/adjectival qualifier 'pure/just'; آورند is again third-person plural present/subjunctive (apodosis/result clause). The clause is the consequence of the previous conditional.
segments.s656-g51: همان is a demonstrative 'that/the same'; باژ و ساوی is a coordinated noun phrase ('baž and sāvi' — here treated as two items/terms). که introduces a relative clause 'which'; فرمودشاه is an inverted poetic sequence of verb + subject (فرمود = said/commanded; شاه = the king) fused in the line, so the relative clause means 'which the king commanded/ordered.'
segments.s656-g52: به خوبی is an adverbial phrase 'with goodness / well / kindly' modifying the verb; فرستم is first-person singular present/future 'I will send / I send'. بران بارگاه is a prepositional/locative phrase 'to that court' (بران = 'to/on that', بارگاه = 'royal court'), placed after the verb. The sentence thus states the speaker's action (I will send) toward that court.
segments.s656-g53: وگر is the conditional 'if / but if'. نامداران ایران is a noun phrase 'the renowned/illustrious men of Iran' (adjective + proper place-name modifier). گروه here follows to mean 'as a group / together' (postposed noun giving the sense 'in a body'). The whole phrase sets up the conditional subject.
segments.s656-g54: ازین is the older orthography of 'از این' = 'from/for this'; دانش 'knowledge/learning' is the reason or domain; آیند is third-person plural present/subjunctive 'they come / should come'. یک سر is an idiom ('one and all', 'every one together') placed before the adjective; ستوه is a predicative adjective 'weary / disgusted'. The clause expresses the expected result: all will be weary.
segments.s656-g55: چو here functions as 'since / when' giving a causal sense; با دانش ما is a prepositional phrase 'with our knowledge' (or 'possessing our learning'); ندارند is third-person plural negative 'they do not have/possess'. تاو is a poetic/unclear item in this line (contextual — likely demonstrative or emphatic), not matching the listed lexical senses and thus left unspecified.
segments.s656-g56: نخواهند is the negative of خواستن in third-person plural (they will not/won't wish or will not do); زین بوم و بر is the stock phrase 'از این بوم و بر / زین بوم و بر' meaning 'from this homeland and region' (here zīn/زین is an older/poetic marker 'from this'); باژ و ساو repeats the coordinated items 'baž and sāw/saav' (earlier mentioned), closing the clause. Word order is verb-final-ish, with the negated verb followed by the locales and the coordinated nouns.
